titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender, 6 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This letter is part of a series of correspondence from Sir Wilfrid Bliss Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War.
In this letter Spender is writing to his wife, Lady Lillian Spender, née Dean, (1880-1968). Sir Wilfrid Spender (1876 - 1960) was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender remained in contact with the Ulster Unionist leadership, and consistently opposed Home Rule and the six-county solution. Lady Lillian served in the Ulster Volunteer Nursing Corps during the war, and was an organiser of the Ulster Division Comforts Fund.
In this letter, Spender tells Lillian that he has spent all day writing his weekly report for the British Cabinet. The report, Spender writes, has been annotated and edited by so many people that it is growing at a worrying rate. Spender compares himself to 'an old hen', expected to lay two eggs for lunch and two before dinner, and notes that 'it is not pride which causes a hen to cackle but rather pleasure at being rid of the thing'. Spender concludes the letter be inquiring after the health of 'Pater'.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
D1633/1/1/377 Aug. 6 Sitting up rather late & I will
follow the example of a p r tho’ I
disapprove of it! I have been busy
all day on my weekly report for
the Cabinet 9 sheets of typed foolscap &
had actually one whole page without
an amendment by the numerous
criteria & got all the body accepted.
Such growth in one of my
numerous offspring makes me anxious
and I wonder if the Chief — —
will now quash it. I am like an
old hen every morning with my
eggs 2 to be laid before lunch
2 before dinner. HM is now
to get 3 a day also! My weekly
and monthly eggs send me out
for a long stout walk so that
I can cackle around the country.
It is not pride that causes a hen
to cackle merely pleasure at being
rid of the thing! An extra A letter from Ivy tells me that
the Pater is ill again. I hope you
always let me know as I am
much more anxious if I think
you don’t. I hear an extra mail
is leaving in a few minutes so
close but will write again Your b w
WB Spender
